config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diprioritaskan tanpa batas, itu mudah berubah jadi pembenaran untuk menekan minoritas atau membungkam suara yang berbeda. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan cara yang tetap menghormati martabat dan kebebasan tiap orang. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari perlindungan hak individu, bukan pengorbanan hak itu begitu saja.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society's interests should come first in some situations, but not as a blanket rule that overrides individual rights. From my perspective as someone from China, I have seen how strong collective priorities can help with stability, public order, and long-term development, and I do value that. But I also think it is dangerous when "the public interest" becomes an excuse to ignore fairness, silence criticism, or treat individuals as disposable. The better principle is balance: society matters, but individual rights must still set real limits on what authorities and majorities can do.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju kalau kepentingan masyarakat dijadikan prioritas di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan kritik, mengabaikan kelompok kecil, atau melegitimasi keputusan yang tidak adil. Memang masyarakat butuh ketertiban dan gotong royong, tetapi itu justru harus dibangun di atas perlindungan hak setiap orang, bukan dengan mengorbankannya begitu saja. Jadi bagi saya, kepentingan sosial penting, tetapi hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas yang tidak boleh dilanggar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically come before the individual, because that easily becomes a blank check for power. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand the value of social order, collective effort, and long-term planning, and I do not dismiss those at all. But I have also seen how "for the common good" can be used too broadly, and once individual rights are treated as secondary in principle, it becomes much easier to silence criticism or excuse unfair treatment. The right balance is not to choose one side absolutely, but to make sure social goals are pursued within clear limits that protect ordinary people.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dalam konteks Indonesia, saya justru melihat betapa sering frasa “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membenarkan kebijakan yang merugikan orang kecil, jadi kalau hak individu tidak jadi batas yang tegas, penyalahgunaannya sangat mudah terjadi. Gotong royong dan ketertiban memang penting, tetapi itu harus berjalan bersama perlindungan hak, bukan menggantikannya. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat dibangun dari keseimbangan, dengan hak individu tetap menjadi pagar utama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as a general rule, because in my view from China, society's interests cannot be used as a permanent excuse to outrank individual rights. I understand why people value stability, order, and collective goals, and I do think those matter for a country to function well, but I have also seen how easily "the common good" can be stretched to justify unfairness or silence dissent. When individual rights are treated as secondary in principle, ordinary people lose a real safeguard against abuse. For me, social interests should be important, but they must operate within firm limits that protect the individual.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
